title: Implement Telemetry Filtering And Metric Aggregation
description: Add focused helper logic in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` to load beads from storage, resolve optional feature-root prefixes, filter by days/feature root/agent type/status, derive wall-clock and turn metrics from execution history, and compute retry/block aggregates needed by both table and JSON output.
acceptance_criteria: Feature-root filtering supports prefix resolution using existing storage resolution behavior.
Wall-clock duration is derived from execution history using started/completed timestamps and skips incomplete terminal entries.

Aggregation includes counts by status, counts by agent type, average turns, average wall-clock, p95 wall-clock, retry rate, corrective bead count, merge-conflict bead count, timeout blocks, and transient blocks.
Corrective and merge-conflict retry detection works from bead ID suffixes.
Aggregation logic returns structured data reusable by multiple output modes.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `_filter_beads_by_days` (days cutoff using first execution_history timestamp), `_bead_wall_clock_seconds` (started→completed/blocked/failed pairs from execution_history, skips incomplete), `_bead_turns` (aggregates num_turns from telemetry artifact JSONs), `_percentile` (linear interpolation p-th percentile), `aggregate_telemetry` (counts by status and agent_type, avg/p95 wall-clock, avg turns, retry rate, corrective count from ID suffix, merge-conflict count, timeout and transient block counts from block_reason), `_format_telemetry_table` (plain-text report), and rewrote `command_telemetry` to apply all filters including `--days`, resolve feature-root prefix via storage, load transient_patterns from config, and route to JSON or table output based on `--json` flag.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — all acceptance criteria satisfied. Tester bead should validate edge cases (empty beads, missing telemetry artifacts, partial execution_history).
handoff_summary.risks: Corrective bead detection relies on bead_id ending with `-corrective` (per acceptance criteria). Transient block detection checks only blocked beads; if a bead was unblocked and retried, its prior transient reason is no longer in status=blocked. These are known constraints per spec.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester bead should verify filtering correctness, wall-clock computation, and aggregate values.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — all changes confined to `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` within the `command_telemetry` block and new helper functions inserted above it. No changes to shared scheduler, storage, or model code.

conflict_risks: None — all changes confined to `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` within the `command_telemetry` block and new helper functions inserted above it. No changes to shared scheduler, storage, or model code.

execution_history.summary: Implemented telemetry filtering and metric aggregation in `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py`. Added `from collections import Counter` and `from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone` imports, then added six helper functions and rewrote `command_telemetry` to use them.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
